page.title: Chronic Kidney Disease — Disease Intelligence | OSMF
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Chronic Kidney Disease: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Chronic Kidney Disease: 100 alterations and 534 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Efficacy and safety of Salvia miltiorrhiza for treating chronic kidney diseases.
Efficacy of probiotics/synbiotics supplementation in patients with chronic kidney disease.
Evidence indicates that exercise attenuates the progression of chronic kidney disease.
High-dose ultra-processed food intake was associated with an increased risk of incident chronic kidney disease.
Whole-body vibration has demonstrated efficacy and feasibility in enhancing muscle strength among patients with chronic kidney disease.
Aspirin did not prevent CV events, with a significantly increased risk of minor bleeding and renal events.
Bisphenol A exposure and kidney diseases: systematic review, meta-analysis, and NHANES 03-16 study.
Efficacy and safety of turmeric dietary supplementation on proteinuria in CKD.
Our findings indicate that high consumption of UPF is significantly associated with an increased risk of CKD.
This study summarizes the potential beneficial effects of dietary fiber in the CKD population demonstrating a reduction in serum urea and creatinine.
25OHD deficiency is a risk factor for secondary hyperparathyroidism and decreased serum 1,25(OH)2D in children with CKD.
A diet rich in vegetables and fruit may reduce the risk for kidney disease.
A flavonoid-rich diet is associated with a lower risk of chronic kidney disease.
A manihot is a promising therapy for patients with primary kidney disease.
A pilot study to examine the effects of an anti-inflammatory supplement on eicosanoid derivatives in patients with chronic kidney disease.
A vitamin D deficiency at baseline is significantly correlated with poorer clinical outcomes and more sever renal pathological features.
Acute effect of photobiomodulation therapy on handgrip strength of chronic kidney disease patients during hemodialysis.
Acute phase proteins and vitamin D seasonal variation in end-stage renal disease patients.
Adequate consumption of phytate can prevent abdominal aortic calcification in patients with chronic kidney disease.
Adherence to the Mediterranean diet has favourable effects on the prevention of kidney dysfunction.
Ascorbic acid lowers central blood pressure and asymmetric dimethylarginine in chronic kidney disease.
Ascorbic acid might be a promising agent in chronic kidney disease for improving outcome by decreasing oxidative stress and ADMA levels.
Association of 1,25 dihydroxyvitamin D with left ventricular hypertrophy and left ventricular diastolic dysfunction in patients with chronic kidney disease.
Association of dietary niacin intake with all-cause and cardiovascular mortality of adult patients with chronic kidney disease.
Associations between long-term exposure to ambient air pollution and renal function.
Beneficial effects of 6-month supplementation with omega-3 acids on selected inflammatory markers in patients with chronic kidney disease.
Breathing training program improves quality of life in patients with predialysis chronic kidney disease.
Combined sodium bicarbonate and ascorbic acid could prevent contrast-induced nephropathy following catheterization in CKD patients.
Consuming nuts 1-6 times per week was associated with a lower prevalence of chronic kidney disease.
Curcumin and Boswellia serrata are safe and tolerable and helped to improvethe levels of an inflammatory cytokine (IL-6).
Curcumin supplementation (Meriva ®) modulates inflammation, lipid peroxidation and gut microbiota composition in chronic kidney disease.
Curcuminoid supplementation for twelve weeks attenuates lipid peroxidation and might reduce uremic toxin in patients with chronic kidney disease undergoing peritoneal dialysis.
Dietary beta-glucans could decrease the levels of the proteolytic uremic toxins p-cresyl sulphate.
Effect of Omega-3 supplements on quality of life among children on dialysis.
Effects of Brazilian green propolis on proteinuria and renal function in patients with chronic kidney disease.
Effects of photobiomodulation in salivary glands of chronic kidney disease patients on hemodialysis.
Elevated exposure to air pollutants accelerates primary glomerular disease rrogression.
Ginsenoside Rb1 treatment was effective in reducing the extent of oxidative stress and inflammation in chronic kidney disease patients.
Hibiscus sabdariffa extract as an adjunct to valsartan in patients with mild chronic kidney disease.
High dietary fiber intake may benefit cognitive function in older patients with chronic kidney disease.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: GFR decline averages 2-5 mL/min/year on standard care (ACE/ARB + blood pressure control); progression to end-stage renal disease (ESRD) in ~20-30% at 10 years for moderate CKD; reversal essentially impossible once structural fibrosis established
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: SGLT2 inhibitors (empagliflozin, dapagliflozin) reduce progression to ESRD by 30-35% in CKD trials (EMPA-KIDNEY, DAPA-CKD); finerenone (non-steroidal MRA) adds 15-20% additional protection; combination slows but does not reverse disease
remission.gap_size: Moderate — SGLT2 inhibitors represent a genuine paradigm shift but are dramatically underused
remission.barrier_detail: SGLT2 inhibitors have robust evidence for CKD protection regardless of diabetes status (EMPA-KIDNEY 2023) but prescribing rates in non-diabetic CKD remain very low; ACE/ARB as sole strategy (established in 1990s-2000s) still dominant despite the new data
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — KDIGO guidelines updated to include SGLT2 inhibitors as first-line in CKD regardless of diabetes (EMPA-KIDNEY data); finerenone added for CKD in T2DM; ACE/ARB first-line unchanged since 2000; this is the first major CKD pharmacotherapy advance in 20 years
remission.outdated_treatments: ACE/ARB monotherapy without SGLT2 inhibitor in eligible CKD patients (KDIGO now recommends SGLT2 inhibitor as co-first-line); protein restriction beyond moderate levels (evidence equivocal, high compliance burden); erythropoiesis-stimulating agents (ESAs) to high hemoglobin targets (increased CV risk — TREAT and CHOIR trials; target now 10-11 g/dL, not 13+)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: SGLT2 inhibitors (just becoming standard, but still underused); low-protein plant-predominant diet (PLADO trial — reduces hyperfiltration, lower acid load); bicarbonate supplementation (slows GFR decline in acidotic CKD — small but real effect); bardoxolone methyl (NRF2 activator, Phase 3 CARDINAL trial positive for CKD in Alport syndrome)
remission.frontier_signal: Sparsentan (dual endothelin/angiotensin antagonist — FDA approved for IgA nephropathy 2023); avacopan (complement inhibitor for ANCA vasculitis CKD); atrasentan (selective endothelin-A, Phase 3 ZENITH trial positive for IgA nephropathy 2024); kidney organoid-based replacement therapy (5-10 year horizon)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
